ausgerechnet.g: of all things, of all people

dreh.g: the knack, hang of it; turn (drehen)

um.g: around; at (a time); in order to

bock.g: to be up for something (Bock haben)

durchmachen.g: to pull an all-nighter, party through (durchmachen)

dazu.g: to it, in addition; to belong

wandert.g: wanders; passes from hand to hand (wandern)

gefallen.g: a favour you do for someone

aufheben.g: to pick up; make a fuss

ton.g: tone, sound; the mood of a group

fischbrötchen.g: a fish sandwich in a bun (Hamburg snack)

aufeinander.g: on each other; at one another

reihum.g: round in turn; one after another

ziert.g: acts coy, plays hard to get (sich zieren)

zug.g: a train; a pull, a drag

ziehen.g: to pull; to move; to drift

kamelle.g: (Cologne) carnival sweets thrown to the crowd

bonbon.g: a sweet, a piece of candy

wirken.g: to seem, come across; to take effect

besorgen.g: to get, sort out (often via contacts)

lebensfreude.g: zest for life, joy of living

schunkeln.g: to sway arm in arm to music

aufgehoben.g: in good hands, well looked after

wegen.g: because of; (von wegen) no way!, as if!

gar.g: at all; even; (with nicht) not at all

ausschank.g: the serving of drinks; a tap, bar

trudeln.g: to trickle in, arrive one by one

nerven.g: to get on one's nerves; nerves

bude.g: a snack stand; a place, room

sturkopf.g: a stubborn so and so, a mule

freuen.g: to be glad, to look forward (sich freuen)

lauthals.g: at the top of one's voice

schrebergarten.g: a rented garden plot with a hut

wiegelt.g: plays it down, waves it off (abwiegeln)

trara.g: a big fuss, song and dance

bagage.g: the whole gang, crew (rough but warm)

mittendrin.g: right in the middle of it

frittenbude.g: a stand selling hot chips and snacks

abpfiff.g: the final whistle of a match

veräppeln.g: to pull someone's leg, to kid

bemme.g: a slice of bread with topping (Saxon)

daneben.g: next to it; beside the point

zettel.g: a slip of paper, a note

fei.g: southern flavoring particle, adds gentle stress

feldweg.g: a narrow dirt track through fields

weges.g: of the path, of the way (Weg, genitive)

gegend.g: the area, part of town around you

sowas.g: such a thing; something like that

anstoßen.g: to clink glasses for a toast

baumeln.g: to dangle; let your soul relax

verglühen.g: to burn out, to fade away glowing

fleischküchle.g: a small Swabian fried meat patty

keineswegs.g: by no means, not at all

gelohnt.g: to be worth it, pay off (lohnen)

vesper.g: a cold snack meal of bread and cheese

aufschnitt.g: cold cuts, sliced meats and cheese

grölen.g: to bawl, to sing loudly and badly

ständchen.g: a serenade, a little song for someone
